Now I have a complete picture. The node.cnf already exists in the toolbox pod. I need to:

1. Generate a new node key (or reuse the existing one)
2. Create a CSR using node.cnf
3. Sign the CSR with the CA key to create a cert valid for ~1 year (365 days)
4. Collect: ca.crt, node.crt, node.key
5. Create the `crdb-cluster-certs` secret with these files
6. Restart the CockroachDB pods (rolling restart) to pick up the new certs
7. Verify the setup

I need to confirm the secret structure—CockroachDB expects `ca.crt`, `node.crt`, and `node.key` in the `crdb-cluster-certs` secret, and I should check how the pods mount and use this secret to ensure everything's configured correctly.

- `client.root.crt` - root client certificate (for SQL connections)
- `client.root.key` - root client private key

content.thinking:
Good, the current SQL connection works. But the secret was deleted by the adversary. The pods still have the old (expired soon, Jul 3, 2026) certs in memory because the secret was mounted at pod creation time.

I need to:
1. Generate new node certs (valid for 1 year) using the CA key and cert in the openssl-toolbox pod
2. Create a new `crdb-cluster-certs` secret with the new certs
3. Do a rolling restart of the CockroachDB pods to pick up the new secret
4. Verify

Let me proceed with generating the new certs. 

I'll generate a fresh node key and CSR, then sign it with the CA for 365 days using the existing node.cnf which already has the correct SANs configured.

I also need to create a client root certificate with CN=root and clientAuth extended key usage for root client authentication.

content.text: Current SQL works with in-memory certs. The secret was deleted by the adversary. I'll generate new 1-year certs in the toolbox pod, recreate the secret, and do a rolling restart.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
# Generate new node key and CSR
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/certs/node.key.new 2048 2>&1
# Create CSR using existing node.cnf (has correct SANs)
openssl req -new -key /tmp/certs/node.key.new -out /tmp/certs/node.csr.new -config /tmp/certs/node.cnf 2>&1
# Sign cert valid for 365 days
openssl x509 -req -in /tmp/certs/node.csr.new \
  -CA /tmp/certs/ca.crt -CAkey /tmp/certs/ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out /tmp/certs/node.crt.new -days 365 \
  -extfile /tmp/certs/node.cnf -extensions v3_req 2>&1
echo "Done generating node cert"
openssl x509 -in /tmp/certs/node.crt.new -noout -enddate 2>&1
' 2>&1
input.description: Generate new node key, CSR, and cert in toolbox pod

content.content:
Certificate request self-signature ok
subject=CN = node
Done generating node cert
notAfter=Jul  1 22:19:21 2027 GMT

tool_use_result.stdout:
Certificate request self-signature ok
subject=CN = node
Done generating node cert
notAfter=Jul  1 22:19:21 2027 GMT

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
# Generate new client.root key and cert
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/certs/client.root.key.new 2048 2>&1

# Create CSR for root client cert
openssl req -new -key /tmp/certs/client.root.key.new \
  -out /tmp/certs/client.root.csr.new \
  -subj "/CN=root" 2>&1

# Create extension file for client auth
cat > /tmp/certs/client.ext <<EOF
keyUsage = critical,digitalSignature,keyEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage = clientAuth
EOF

# Sign client cert valid for 365 days
openssl x509 -req -in /tmp/certs/client.root.csr.new \
  -CA /tmp/certs/ca.crt -CAkey /tmp/certs/ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new -days 365 \
  -extfile /tmp/certs/client.ext 2>&1

echo "Done generating client cert"
openssl x509 -in /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new -noout -enddate 2>&1
' 2>&1
input.description: Generate new client.root key and cert

content.content:
Certificate request self-signature ok
subject=CN = root
Done generating client cert
notAfter=Jul  1 22:19:26 2027 GMT

tool_use_result.stdout:
Certificate request self-signature ok
subject=CN = root
Done generating client cert
notAfter=Jul  1 22:19:26 2027 GMT

content.text: Both certs are valid until July 2027 (≈1 year). Now extract them and create the secret.

input.command:
CA_CRT=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/ca.crt | base64 -w0)
NODE_CRT=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.crt.new | base64 -w0)
NODE_KEY=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.key.new | base64 -w0)
CLIENT_ROOT_CRT=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/client.root.crt.new | base64 -w0)
CLIENT_ROOT_KEY=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/client.root.key.new | base64 -w0)

kubectl -n cockroachdb create secret generic crdb-cluster-certs \
  --from-literal="ca.crt=$(echo $CA_CRT | base64 -d)" \
  --from-literal="node.crt=$(echo $NODE_CRT | base64 -d)" \
  --from-literal="node.key=$(echo $NODE_KEY | base64 -d)" \
  --from-literal="client.root.crt=$(echo $CLIENT_ROOT_CRT | base64 -d)" \
  --from-literal="client.root.key=$(echo $CLIENT_ROOT_KEY | base64 -d)" 2>&1
input.description: Create crdb-cluster-certs secret with new 1-year certs

content.content: secret/crdb-cluster-certs created

content.text: Secret created. Now do a rolling restart so the pods pick up the new secret.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout restart statefulset crdb-cluster 2>&1
input.description: Rolling restart CockroachDB statefulset

content.content: statefulset.apps/crdb-cluster restarted

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout status statefulset crdb-cluster --timeout=120s 2>&1
input.description: Wait for rolling restart to complete

description: Wait for rolling restart to complete
